food_subgroup: Filled sweet buns and enriched bread rolls

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific entry for snow mountain bun or Mexican-topping cream cheese bun; 'sweet bun' (FOODON_03302397) is the closest defensible parent class.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKY covers sweet buns and rolls as a category; no specific code exists for snow mountain bun with cream cheese filling.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept exists for this specific bakery product.

serving.natural_language_portion: One snow mountain bun weighing approximately 90 g, as typically served at a Chinese bakery in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Cream-filled enriched sweet bun — estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for enriched sweet rolls with cream cheese filling and Mexican-style sweet bread topping
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central (SR Legacy)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA Branded Foods for this specific product. Values are estimated by component weighting: enriched sweet yeast dough (USDA SR Legacy #18027, sweet rolls with raisins adapted for plain milk dough), cream cheese filling (USDA SR Legacy #01017), and Mexican sweet bread topping (pan dulce analogue, USDA SR Legacy #18420). Proportions estimated as approximately 60% dough, 25% cream cheese filling, 15% crispy topping by weight. All values are per 100 g of finished bun.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of white bread and enriched sweet bread products — analogous estimate based on white bread GI (75) and sweet enriched rolls with fat and dairy filling (GI ~55–70 range)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for snow mountain bun. GI estimated at 65 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for enriched white bread rolls (GI 73) and sweet buns with fat and dairy filling (GI ~55–65). Fat and protein content from cream cheese filling and butter lower the GI relative to plain white bread. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 40.95) / 100 ≈ 26.6, rounded to 26.5. Serving carbohydrate = 45.5 g per 100 g × 0.90 kg serving = 40.95 g.

hypertension.guidance: Each bun contributes approximately 252 mg sodium (280 mg per 100 g × 90 g serving), which represents about 11% of the 2300 mg daily sodium limit recommended by the American Heart Association; individuals with hypertension should limit intake to one bun and avoid pairing with other high-sodium foods.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate and sugar content with medium glycemic index
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of 65 (medium) and a glycemic load of approximately 26.5 per bun, this product causes a significant postprandial glucose rise; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this as an occasional indulgence, limit to half a bun, and pair with a protein or fibre source to blunt the glycemic response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and trans fat from butter and cream cheese
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: One bun provides approximately 7.7 g saturated fat and 0.18 g trans fat, representing a meaningful proportion of the daily saturated fat limit of 20 g for a 2000 kcal diet; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption to occasional treats and prioritise lower-fat bakery alternatives.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Milk Snow Mount Bun (雪山包, Xuě Shān Bāo) is a Chinese-style enriched sweet bun popular in Singapore's Chinese bakery scene. It is made using the sponge dough method, which involves a two-stage fermentation process that produces an exceptionally soft, fluffy, and pillowy crumb with a fine, tender texture. The bun is filled with a rich cream cheese filling that delivers a tangy, creamy, and milky flavour profile. The exterior is coated with a sweet Mexican-style crispy topping — a buttery, sugar-enriched crust that bakes into a crackled, lightly crunchy shell reminiscent of a Mexican concha or Hong Kong pineapple bun topping. The combination of the soft milk dough, the creamy filling, and the crispy topping creates a multi-textural eating experience. Nutritionally, the bun is energy-dense at approximately 342 kcal per 100 g, with significant contributions from refined carbohydrates (45.5 g), fat (14.8 g, of which 8.5 g is saturated), and moderate protein (7.2 g) from the dairy components. The sugar content is high at 16 g per 100 g, and the glycemic index is estimated at 65 (medium), making it a high-glycemic-load food per serving. It is best consumed fresh on the day of purchase. Clinically, this bun is a high-calorie, high-saturated-fat, high-sugar bakery product that should be consumed in moderation by individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or hypertension. It is vegetarian but not vegan, and contains gluten, dairy, and egg allergens.

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional entry exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods, or ICMR-NIN for this specific product. Values are estimated by component weighting from USDA SR Legacy entries for enriched sweet dough, cream cheese, and Mexican sweet bread topping. The estimation methodology is transparent and defensible but introduces uncertainty of approximately ±10–15% on macronutrient values. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from 'Beverages' (as listed in the field survey source) to 'Snacks' — this is clearly a filled sweet bun, not a beverage; the original classification appears to be a data entry error in the field survey platform. 2. Vitamin C is 0.0 — confirmed zero as this baked dairy-and-wheat product contains no significant vitamin C source; naturally zero by ingredient composition. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy component sources used for estimation. 6. Trans fat is estimated at 0.2 g per 100 g based on butter content; this is a low-confidence estimate. 7. Halal status is marked false as the restaurant (RUXU 入续 糖水铺与包) does not appear to carry a Muis halal certification based on available public information; this should be verified. 8. GI estimated at 65 based on analogous enriched sweet bread entries from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct measurement exists for this product. 9. Serving size estimated at 90 g based on typical Chinese bakery bun portion sizes; actual weight may vary. 10. glycemic_index set to 65 (medium GI) based on analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database for enriched sweet rolls with fat and dairy filling.
